(ii)
Pyruvic acid + (i) + NAD* — Acetyl COA +
Enzyme
(iii) + NAD + H* (Pg 231, E)
A) (i) 0, (ii) Mg2* (iii) CO,
B) (i) 0, (ii) Na (iii) H,0
C) (i) CoA (ii) Na (iii) CO,
D) (i) CoA (i) Mg2* (ii) CO,
51. Pyruvate enters to the mitochondrial
matrix and undergoes. (Pg 231, E)
A) Reductive decarboxylation
B) Oxidative carboxylation
C) Reductive carboxylation
D) Oxidative decarboxylation
52. Which enzyme catalyse the reaction going
on in mitochondrial matrix in respiration?
(Pg 231, E)
A) Pyruvate carboxylase
B) Lactate dehydrogenase
C) Alcohol dehydrogenase
D) Pyruvate dehydrogenase
53. Who elucidated Tricarboxylic Acid cycle?
(Pg 231, E)
A) Johns Elen B) Hans Krebs
C) Meyerhoff D) Elena Parker
54. Formation of Acetyl coenzyme A from
Pyruvate in mitochondrial matrix yields
which among the following? (Pg 231, E)
A) CO B) H20
C) NADPH + H* D) Both A and C
55. How many molecules of NADH + H- are
produced when pyruvate converts to
Acetyl CoA in TCA cycle? (Pg 231, E)
A) 0 B) 1
C)2 D) 3
14.4.1 ‘Tricarboxylic Acid Cycle’
56. Where does TCA cycle occurs? (Pg 231, E)
A) Cytoplasm
B) Mitochondria cell wall
C) Mitochondrial matrix
D) Chloroplast
57. What is the first product of TCA cycle?
(Pg 231, E)
A) Acetyl CoA B) Citric acid
C) Isocitric acid D) OAA
58. What is the 1ststep of TCA cycle?
(Pg 231, E)
A) Formation of citrate from isocitrate
